Alright, you're back. Let's roll forward here with your next lesson on body language, which is called the space bubble. Now, here's the deal. As you move through the world, there's a certain bubble around you and if people invade that bubble, you tend to feel really uncomfortable to the point where it actually breaks your form. Now, I have a time for this that I have coined, but I want you to first watch this clip of an old video game, test your knowledge here, check out this clip and tell me if you know what it is. Alright, now without going on the internet and cheating, I want you to tell me if A, this video game is Ms. Pac-Man, B, is it Asteroids, C, is it Space Invaders, or D, is it Galaga? Really, you think this is Ms. Pac-Man? No, no, this is Ms. Pac-Man. Check it out. I'll talk while you watch. You see this little thing here, this little lady here, the thing, she's actually eating all the blue meanings, that's Ms. Pac-Man. The correct answer is Space Invaders. No, no, no, it's not Asteroids. Asteroids, by the way, is my favorite video game growing up. Here's the Asteroids. I'm gonna talk while you watch it. You see this little ship? That's you here. These are your Asteroids, kind of old, I know it's old school, right? But your cores around, your shoot little bolts, these ships, you have a hyperspace button. That's Asteroids. The correct answer is Space Invaders. No, it's not Galaga, but Galaga's pretty cool. Make some wild noises here. Check this out. Talk as you watch. You can hear these wacky noises. This is Galaga. You're the ship here. You're shooting at these dive bombers at you. That's your basic Galadier aim. The correct answer is actually Space Invaders. Boom, you hit it on the head. It is Space Invaders. You see what's happening here, and put that back up. You're gonna show the graphic here. Check this out. You're the guy at the bottom here, and these ships are just encroaching on you and encroaching on you. And if they get too close, they break the bubble, so to speak. Bam, you lose. Hence the term Space Invaders. Hopefully you'll never forget that term again. Listen, the way this works is as you move through the world, you have a space around you, as does everybody else, especially your prospects. And if you go inside that bubble when you're in person, below two and a half max, but really three, two and a half in the Western world, we'll discuss the Eastern world a bit later, but three feet, two and a half minimum. Go inside that, break rapport on a very deep fundamental level to the point where people will actually back up, and they'll grimace. They'll make a weird expression because it's very uncomfortable when you have your space bubble violated. In fact, let's watch this right now at a live event. I have two people on stage and watch what happens when someone's bubble gets invaded. Did you really enjoy this? So when I'm trying to influence what I, I will always, when I talk to them, I'm always conscious that I'll stay face to face. Now look how far I am. I'll always stay what? Three, at least three feet away. As soon as I go here, what happens? How do you feel? It's weird. So this is the next one is called invading someone's space bubble. We talk, how are you doing right here? We come as soon as I go here, what do you feel? You're like, oh no, it's called the space invader. All right, now you have it. So you see the faces, the expressions, and just like, you know, back up. It's, it's palpable when someone invades your space bubble, you get this feeling, there's just something wrong. Usually there's spitters too, you know, kind of watch that's pretty gross. Okay, so please make sure that you don't become one of those space invaders, right? Because it's really hard to close when you're not in rapport. In fact, it's impossible. So you cannot invade the space bubble. Now one point here, I brought up the Eastern world, right? Asia. Well, if you ever travel, they're done business there, you'll notice people tend to stand closer together. The space bubble there is actually tighter. Not by much though, only six inches, but it's profound. So really, it's two, two and a half feet in the Eastern world, Eastern culture, right? That's Asia, Japan, China, right? So just be aware that when you're doing business in that part of the world, you can walk a bit close, but more importantly, don't be turned off if someone encroaches in your bubble. In other words, if you're the salesperson and a prospect walks in your bubble and you clam up and feel weird, that's going to cause them to feel something, they'll break rapport like that. Just remember, when you're in that part of the world, we must do as the Romans do, right? So you want to make sure you allow yourself that extra six inches of leeway wall on the receiving end that you don't like flip out and you'll be fine. Okay? So that's the deal with invading the space bubble. It's profound. Simple lesson, don't do it. Okay? Now let's move on to the next lesson here, which is the differences between men and women. And this is a perfect time for us now to cut back to live footage again. Because I do a great demonstration. I call a man and a woman that kind of young like early, late teens, early 20s. Just watch the differences between the two and you'll notice how even when one person tries to break those rules of body language, the other person naturally will move away. It's really profound. Once you're aware of it, you can watch it happen. Hopefully this will allow you to lock this stuff in right here. And then on the other side when done watching, we'll discuss it. I'll give you some pointers and we'll move on. So man and woman, right? Example, okay? When a man, if I'm going to be standing influencing him, okay, if we're going to talk together, I would never ever say how you do and watch. I would never go to him and talk. I would always go off to an angle. If we're going to be standing up, I never would stand directly face to face with another man. So watch, come here. He stands as you are, square of me, stand square, no square of me like this. Okay, you know, so he almost didn't want to do it. He's almost against me. He was almost stopping himself from standing square at me because he says, if I go to this, how do you feel right now with me? Pretty awkward, right? Right. It's almost intimidating. Now, how do you feel like now when we talk? More casual. Anytime a man is going to influence a man, as soon as you square off with a man, it's conflict. It's immediately conflict. I was saying, he's like, no, I don't want to try to back off. You'll notice, and I've seen this happen when I actually am training salesmen, I'm observing, if the salesman tries to use bad body language and tries to square off with a man, okay, the prospect will back off and corner off. And sometimes the guy will keep trying to chase the guy around. It's the most bizarre thing you've ever seen. Okay, but now watch out. Go stand next to her, stand with her. How would you, how would you guys go, go start a talk? Look what they did. How are they? They squared off. Instantly squared off. Why is that? 100 out of 100 times that will happen. Every single time a man and a woman, if you say face to that will square off with each other, because he knows intuitively smart, I guess a good, unless a person's terrible body language, right, really negative byage, that a woman wants to see the man. The woman does not want the man off to the, the woman's like, what the fuck is he trying to do, right? The woman wants the man to square off. So whenever a man, here, move to the center of the stage, yeah. Okay. So if a man is influencing a woman, you want to stand face to face, or if you're at a table sitting across a table, you want to sit across from a woman. You don't want to sit to the side of a woman. The woman will be naturally, when it doesn't raises up her guard and makes it harder to slide into rapport with someone. All right, so there you have it. The differences between men and women. Just remember, this includes standing up, sitting down, all aspects of communication. And this is also one of the things that's so easy to abide by. Just if you find yourself in a situation when you're sitting face to face. So when even like in an interview, all you got to do is casually turn your chair, just like 20 degrees to one side, if it's man to man, and you'll get out of that sort of face to face confrontation. Same thing with women here. If you find yourself at a corner, just make sure you move so you're facing, keep your hands above the table if you're sitting. Simple stuff to follow. The thing about body language, this is that good enough factor stuff. Meaning you just learn it, it's easy, it's not hard to do. And once you apply it in the real world, the differences come hard and fast, and you'll see it. You get it? It's not going to have to wait for this to happen.
